name: Shared PDE5 inhibition supports plausibility but not outcome equivalence
statement: Sildenafil, tadalafil, vardenafil, and avanafil share PDE5 inhibition and NO/cGMP-mediated vasodilatory pharmacology, so non-tadalafil PDE5 evidence can support class-level plausibility; it cannot by itself establish tadalafil-specific clinical effects for cognition, mood, cardiovascular prevention, exercise, or longevity.
population_or_context: Class-level PDE5 inhibitor pharmacology applied to off-label tadalafil evidence synthesis.
time_horizon: Baseline framework current to June 6, 2026 source check.

source_locator.section: NCBI Bookshelf PDE5 inhibitors overview and label mechanism sections
source_locator.quote_or_paraphrase_note: Overview describes PDE5 inhibitors as blocking cGMP breakdown and prolonging nitric-oxide mediated vasodilation; labels describe agent-specific PDE5 inhibition.

caveats: Mechanistic plausibility is not clinical efficacy.
The same class mechanism can produce both hypothesized benefits and safety risks.
Outcome transfer depends on dose, timing, population, and endpoint context.
population_profile: Mechanism applies broadly to PDE5 inhibitor users but does not directly measure mostly healthy men age 30 to 50 using tadalafil off label.
tadalafil_exposure: Class-level PDE5 exposure including tadalafil, sildenafil, vardenafil, and avanafil; no specific tadalafil dose tested in this finding.
class_transfer_limits: Use as background plausibility only unless a tadalafil-specific or molecule-bridged clinical source measures the target endpoint under comparable exposure conditions.
indication_context: Mechanistic class overview and label pharmacology, not off-label treatment evidence.
comparator_context: No clinical comparator; class mechanism comparison.
endpoint_measure: Shared PDE5 inhibition and NO/cGMP pathway pharmacology.
effect_estimate: Qualitative: shared PDE5 mechanism supports plausibility but not agent equivalence.
safety_findings: The shared mechanism also supports vasodilatory interaction concerns with nitrates, GC stimulators, alpha-blockers, antihypertensives, and alcohol.
directness_to_target_population: High for class-mechanism classification; low for healthy-user clinical outcome inference.
confounding_and_bias_limits: Mechanistic summaries can overstate clinical transfer when outcomes depend on tissue distribution, exposure duration, or disease context.
